items.title: Iowa State's Lipsey injures knee, out 4-6 weeks
items.summary: Iowa State starting point guard Tamin Lipsey will miss four to six weeks after injuring his knee at practice Tuesday, the school announced.

items.title: Virginia Tech proposes adding $229M for athletics
items.summary: Virginia Tech has proposed adding an extra $229 million to its athletics budget over the next four years to help position the Hokies to compete with the top programs in the ACC, a crucial moment with the hire of a new football coach looming.

items.title: Loyola Ramblers chaplain Sister Jean announces retirement due to health concerns
items.summary: Jean Dolores Schmidt, who is 106, rose to prominence during Loyola Chicago's epic 2018 NCAA tournament Final Four run.

items.title: NCAA grants former G League player eligibility
items.summary: NCAA ruled that former G League Ignite player Thierry Darlan, 21, is eligible to play Division I basketball at Santa Clara, his agent told ESPN.

items.title: Early men's Bracketology: Houston stays No. 1, Virginia gives ACC hope
items.summary: The new Cavaliers coach is tasked with winning as big as the coach he once beat in a historic upset. Can it be done in Year 1?

items.title: The No. 1 men's hoops recruit at every position in the 2026 class -- and his competition
items.summary: Five positions, five potential No. 1s at each in the 2026 class. Which high school prospects are in the mix?

items.title: Okla. State lands No. 44 hoops recruit Montonati
items.summary: Senior Jalen Montonati, the No. 44 player in the 2026 SportsCenter NEXT 100 basketball rankings, announced his commitment to Oklahoma State.

items.title: How Kansas beat Louisville for five-star recruit Taylen Kinney, more commit fits
items.summary: Breaking down the fits of every top class of 2026 commitment, including Kansas' first five-star of the cycle.

items.title: PG Kinney, No. 17 hoops recruit, picks Jayhawks
items.summary: Five-star senior Taylen Kinney, the No. 4 point guard in the 2026 SportsCenter NEXT rankings, announced his commitment to Kansas.

items.title: Hakeem Olajuwon's son, Aziz, picks Stanford
items.summary: Aziz Olajuwon, a top-40 recruit and the son of NBA Hall of Famer Hakeem Olajuwon, has committed to Stanford, he told ESPN on Saturday.

items.title: 'Be a coach': Dan Hurley on ego, Maui losses, Lakers -- and Geno Auriemma's wake-up call
items.summary: In Hurley's "Never Stop: Life, Leadership, and What It Takes To Be Great," he writes that he reached out to Auriemma after UConn's 0-3 trip to the Maui Invitational last November.

items.title: Men's college basketball coaching changes for 2025-26
items.summary: An up-to-date list of every coaching vacancy in men's college basketball ahead of the 2025-26 season.

items.title: Pearl steps down after 11 seasons at Auburn
items.summary: Bruce Pearl has stepped down as the men's hoops coach at Auburn and will move into an ambassador's role in the athletic department as special assistant to the athletics director, the school announced Monday.

items.title: What Bruce Pearl's retirement means for Auburn, college basketball
items.summary: The Tigers' winningest coach is retiring. Here's what that means with 42 days until the 2025-26 season tips off.

items.title: UF restores Maxwell's stats, including points mark
items.summary: The University of Florida has restored basketball great Vernon Maxwell's final two years of college statistics, a move that once again makes him the program's all-time leading scorer.
